verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then said Jesus to those Jews which believed on Him, If ye continue in My word, then are ye My disciples indeed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Beloved, our condition needs much endurance; and endurance is produced when doctrines are deeply rooted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as no wind is able by its assaults to tear up the oak, which sends down its root into the lower recesses of the earth, and is firmly clenched there; so too the soul which is nailed by the fear of God none will be able to overturn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since to be nailed is more than to be rooted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus the Prophet prayeth, saying, “Nail my flesh by Thy fear” ( Ps. cxix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 120 , LXX.); “do Thou so fix and join me, as by a nail riveted into me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as men of this kind are hard to be captured, so the opposite sort are a ready prey, and are easily thrown down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As was the case of the Jews at that time; for after having heard and believed, they again turned out of the way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ therefore desiring to deepen their faith that it might not be merely superficial, diggeth into their souls by more striking words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was the part of believers to endure even reproofs, but they immediately were wroth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how doth He this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He first telleth them, “If ye continue in My word, ye are My disciples indeed: and the truth shall make you free.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All but saying, “I am about to make a deep incision, but be not ye moved”; or rather by these expressions He allayed the pride of their imagination.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Shall make you free”: from what, tell me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From your sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then say those boasters?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We be Abraham’s seed, and were never in bondage to any man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Immediately their imagination dropped, and this happened from their having been fluttered about worldly things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If ye continue in My word,” was the expression of One declaring what was in their heart, and knowing that they had indeed believed, but had not continued.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He promiseth a great thing, that they should become His disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since some had gone away from Him before this, alluding to them He saith, “If ye continue,” because they also had heard and believed, and departed because they could not continue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For many of His disciples went back, and walked no more openly with Him.” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye shall know the truth,” that is, “shall know Me, for I am the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All the Jewish matters were types, but ye shall know the truth from Me, and it shall free you from your sins.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As to those others He said, “Ye shall die in your sins,” so to these He saith, “shall make you free.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “I will deliver you from bondage,” this He allowed them to conjecture.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then said they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We be Abraham’s seed, and were never in bondage to any man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet if they must needs have been vexed, it might have been expected that they would have been so at the former part of His speech, at His having said, “Ye shall know the truth”; and that they would have replied, “What! do we not now know the truth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is then the Law and our knowledge a lie?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they cared for none of these things, they are grieved at worldly things, and these were their notions of bondage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And certainly even now, there are many who feel shame at indifferent matters, and at this kind of bondage, but who feel none for the bondage of sin, and who would rather be called servants to this latter kind of bondage ten thousand times, than once to the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such were these men, and they did not even know of any other bondage, and they say, “Bondsmen callest thou those who are of the race of Abraham, the nobly born, who therefore ought not to be called bondsmen?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, saith one, we were never in bondage to any man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such are the boastings of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We are the seed of Abraham,” “we are Israelites.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They never mention their own righteous deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore John cried out to them, saying, “Think not to say that we have Abraham to our father.” ( Matt. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why did not Christ confute them, for they had often been in bondage to the Egyptians, Babylonians, and many others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because His words were not to gain honor for Himself, but for their salvation, for their benefit, and toward this object He was pressing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He might have spoken of the four hundred years, He might have spoken of the seventy, He might have spoken of the years of bondage during the time of the Judges, at one time twenty, at another two, at another seven; He might have said that they had never ceased being in bondage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He desired not to show that they were slaves of men, but that they were slaves of sin, which is the most grievous slavery, from which God alone can deliver; for to forgive sins belongeth to none other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this too they allowed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then they confessed that this was the work of God, He bringeth them to this point, and saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing that this is the freedom of which He speaketh, the freedom from this service.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The servant abideth not in the house, but the Son abideth forever.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Gently too from this He casts down the things of the Law, alluding to former times.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that they may not run back to them and say, “We have the sacrifices which Moses commanded, they are able to deliver us,” He addeth these words, since otherwise what connection would the saying have?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God, being justified freely by His grace” ( Rom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 23, 24 ), even the priests themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul also saith of the priest, that “he ought as for the people so also for himself to offer for sins, for that he also is compassed about with infirmity.” ( Heb. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is signified by His saying, “The servant abideth not in the house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here also He showeth His equal honor with the Father, and the difference between slave and free.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the parable has this meaning, that is, “the servant hath no power,” this is the meaning of “abideth not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why when speaking of sins doth He mention a “house”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is to show that as a master hath power over his house, so He over all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the, “abideth not,” is this,“hath not power to grant favors, as not being master of the house”; but the Son is master of the house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the, “abideth forever,” by a metaphor drawn from human things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That they may not say, “who art thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “All is Mine, (He saith,) for I am the Son, and dwell in My Father’s house,” calling by the name of “house” His power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As in another place He calleth the Kingdom His Father’s house, “In My Father’s house are many mansions.” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since the discourse was of freedom and bondage, He with reason used this metaphor, telling them that they had no power to set free.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If the Son therefore shall make you free.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou the consubstantiality of the Son with the Father, and how He declareth that He hath the same power as the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If the Son make you free, no man afterwards gain-sayeth, but ye have firm freedom.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “it is God that justifieth, who is He that condemneth?” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He showeth that He Himself is pure from sin, and alludeth to that freedom which reached only to a name; this even men give, but that God alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so he persuaded them not to be ashamed at this slavery, but at that of sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And desiring to show that they were not slaves, except by repudiating that liberty, He the more showeth them to be slaves by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye shall be free indeed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is the expression of one declaring that this freedom was not real.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, that they might not say, “We have no sin,” (for it was probable that they would say so,) observe how He bringeth them beneath this imputation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For omitting to convict all their life, He bringeth forward that which they had in hand, which they yet desired to do, and saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I know that ye are Abraham’s seed but ye seek to kill Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Gently and by little doth He expel them from that relationship, teaching them not to be high-minded because of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as freedom and bondage depend on men’s actions, so also doth relationship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not directly, “Ye are not the seed of Abraham, ye the murderers of the righteous”; but for a while He even goeth along with them, and saith, “I know that ye are Abraham’s seed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet this is not the matter in question, and during the remainder of this speech He useth greater vehemence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we may for the most part observe, that when He is about to work any great thing, after He hath wrought it, He useth greater boldness of speech, as though the testimony from His works shut men’s mouths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But ye seek to kill Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What of that,” saith some one, “if they sought to do so justly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this was not so either; wherefore also He puts the reason;

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because My word hath no place in you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then was it,” saith some one, “that they believed on Him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As I before said, they changed again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account He touched them sharply.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If ye boast the relationship of Abraham ye ought also to show forth his life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He said not, “Ye do not contain my words,” but, “My word hath no place in you,” thus declaring the sublimity of His doctrines.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not for this ought they to have slain, but rather to have honored and waited on Him so as to learn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But what,” saith some one, “if thou speakest these things of thyself?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I speak that which I have seen with My Father, and ye do that which ye have heard from your father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “As,” He saith, “I both by My words and by the truth declare the Father, so also do ye by your actions (declare yours).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I have not only the same Substance, but also the same Truth with the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They said unto Him, Abraham is our father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus saith unto them, If ye had Abraham to your father, ye would do the works of Abraham.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now ye seek to kill Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He here repeatedly handleth their murderous intention, and maketh mention of Abraham.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this He doth desiring to draw off their attention from this relationship, and to take away their excessive boasting, and also to persuade them no longer to rest their hopes of salvation in Abraham, nor in the relationship which is according to nature, but in that which is according to the will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what hindered their coming to Christ was this, their deeming that relationship to be sufficient for them to salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is the “truth” of which He speaketh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That He is equal with the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was on this account that the Jews sought to slay Him; and He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye seek to kill Me because I have told you the truth, which I have heard of My Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To show that these things are not opposed to the Father, He again betaketh Himself to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They say unto Him,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We be not born of fornication, we have one Father, even God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What sayest thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye have God for your Father, and do ye blame Christ for asserting this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He said that God was His Father in a special manner?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore He had cast them out of their relationship to Abraham, having nothing to reply, they dare a greater thing, and betake themselves to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But from this honor also He expelleth them, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If God were your Father, ye would love Me; for I proceeded forth and came from God; neither came I of Myself, but He sent Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why do ye not understand My speech?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Even because ye cannot hear My word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye are of your father the devil, and the lusts of your father ye will do: he was a murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth: when he speaketh a lie, he speaketh of his own.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He had driven them out of their relationship to Abraham, and when they dared greater things, He then addeth a blow, telling them that they not only are not Abraham’s children, but that they are even children of the devil, and inflicting a wound which might counterbalance their shamelessness; nor doth He leave it unsupported, but establisheth it by proofs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For,” He saith, “to murder belongeth to the wickedness of the devil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He said not merely, “ye do his works,” but, “ye do his lusts,” showing that both he and they hold to murder, and that envy was the cause.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the devil destroyed Adam, not because he had any charge against him, but only from envy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To this also He alludeth here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And abode not in the truth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, in the right life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since they continually accused Him of not being from God, He telleth them that this also is from thence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the devil first was the father of a lie, when he said, “In the day that ye eat thereof your eyes shall be opened” ( Gen. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ), and he first used it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For men use a lie not as a thing proper, but alien to their nature, but he as proper.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And because I tell you the truth, ye believe Me not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What kind of consequence is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Having no charge against Me, ye desire to kill Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For because ye are enemies of the truth, therefore ye persecute Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since had this not been the reason, ye would have named your charge.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Which of you convinceth Me of sin?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then they said, “We be not born of fornication.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet in fact many of them were born of fornication, for they practiced unbefitting unions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still He doth not convict them of this, but setteth Himself to the other point.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He hath proved them to be, not of God, but of the devil, by all these signs, (for to do murder is of the devil, and to lie is of the devil, both which ye do,) then He showeth that to love is the sign of being of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why do ye not understand My speech?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since they were always doubting, saying, “What is it that he saith, ‘Whither I go ye cannot come’?” therefore He telleth them, “Ye do not understand My speech,” “because ye have not the word of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this cometh to you, because that your understanding is groveling, and because what is Mine is far too great for you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what if they could not understand?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not to be able here means not to be willing; for “ye have trained yourselves to be mean, to imagine nothing great.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they said that they persecuted Him as being themselves zealous for God, on this account He everywhere striveth to show that to persecute Him is the act of those who hate God, but that, on the contrary, to love Him is the act of those who know God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “We have one Father, even God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this ground they pride themselves, on their honor not their righteous deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore your not believing is no proof that I am an enemy to God, but your unbelief is a sign that you do not know God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the reason is, from your being willing to lie and to do the works of the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this is the effect of meanness of soul; (as the Apostle saith, ‘For whereas there is among you envying and strife, are ye not carnal?’) ( 1 Cor. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why is it that ye cannot ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because ye will to do the lusts of your father, ye are eager, ye are ambitious (to do them).”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that “ye cannot” express a want of will?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “this did not Abraham.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What are his works?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Gentleness, meekness, obedience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But ye set yourselves on the contrary part, being hard and cruel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how came it into their thoughts to betake themselves to God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He had shown them unworthy of Abraham; desiring therefore to escape this charge, they mounted higher.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He reproached them with murder, they said this, making it, as it were, a kind of excuse for themselves that they were avenging God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He showeth that this very thing is the act of men opposing God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the, “I came forth,” showeth that He was from thence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith, “I came forth,” alluding to His arrival among us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But since they would probably say to Him, “Thou speaketh certain things strange and new,” He telleth them that He was come from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And therefore with good reason ye hear them not, because ye are of the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For on what account would ye kill Me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What charge have ye to bring against Me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If there be none, why do ye not believe Me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus then having proved them to be of the devil by their lying and their murder, He showeth them also to be alien from Abraham and from God, both because they hated One who had done no wrong, and because they would not hear His word; and in every way He proveth that He was not opposed to God, and that it was not on this account that they refused to believe, but because they were aliens from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when One who had done no sin, who said that He came from God and was sent of God, who spake the truth, and so spake it as to challenge all to the proof, after this was not believed, it is clear that He was not believed because of their being carnal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since sins do use, yea they do use to debase a soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore It saith, “Seeing ye are become dull of hearing.” ( Heb. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when a man cannot despise earthly things, how shall he ever be wise concerning heavenly things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, I exhort you, use we every means that our life may be righteous, that our minds may be cleansed, so that no filthiness be a hindrance to us; kindle for yourselves the light of knowledge, and sow not among thorns.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For how shall one who knows not that covetousness is an evil, ever know the greater good? how shall one who refrains not from these earthly things ever hold fast to those heavenly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is good to take by violence, not the things that perish, but the Kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The violent,” it saith, “take it by force.” ( Matt. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is then not possible to attain to it by sluggishness, but by zeal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what meaneth “the violent”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There is need of much violence, (for strait is the way,) there is need of a youthful soul and a noble.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Plunderers desire to outstrip all other, they look to nothing, neither to conviction, nor accusation, nor punishment, but are given up to one thing only, the getting hold of what they desire to seize, and they run past all that are before them in the way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seize we then the Kingdom of heaven, for here to seize is no fault but rather praise, and the fault is the not seizing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here our wealth comes not from another’s loss.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Haste we then to seize it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Should passion disquiet us, should lust disquiet us, let us do violence to our nature, let us become more gentle, let us labor a little, that we may rest forever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seize not thou gold, but seize that wealth which showeth gold to be but mud.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For tell me, if lead and gold were laid before thee, which wouldest thou take?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it not clear that thou wouldest take the gold?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou then, where one who seizes is punished, prefer that which is the more valuable, but where one who seizes is honored, give up what is the more valuable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If there were punishment in both cases, wouldest thou not rather aim at this latter ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in this case there is nothing like punishment, but even blessedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, “How,” saith some one, “may one seize it?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Cast away the things which thou hast already in thy hands; for so long as thou graspest them thou wilt not be able to seize the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For consider, I pray you, a man with his hands full of silver, will he be able, as long as he retains it, to seize on gold, unless he first cast away the silver, and be free?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because he that seizes a thing must be well-girt so as not to be detained.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And even now there are adverse powers running down against us to rob us, but let us fly them, let us fly them, trailing after us nothing that may give a hold, let us cut asunder the cords, let us strip ourselves of the things of earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What need of silken garments?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long shall we be unrolling this mockery?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long shall we be burying gold?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I desired to cease from always saying these things, but ye will not suffer me, continually supplying me with occasions and arguments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now at least let us desist, that having instructed others by our lives, we may obtain the promised good things, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, now and ever and world without end.
